[File: 'src/interfaces/IMidnight.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized + take] Can an unprivileged taker, under a multicall that first calls setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim) via an already-authorized operator and then immediately calls take() on a sell offer on behalf of victim, bypass the TakerUnauthorized check and open debt on victim's position without victim's per-action consent, violating the invariant that debt increases require explicit per-action authorization? [File: 'src/interfaces/IMidnight.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setConsumed + take] Can an attacker who is the maker of a group-capped offer compose a multicall of setConsumed(group, 0, maker) followed immediately by take() on the same offer, resetting consumed[maker][group] to zero mid-transaction and allowing the offer to be filled beyond its original maxUnits or maxAssets cap, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity? [File: 'src/interfaces/IMidnight.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + repay + withdrawCollateral] Can a borrower compose a multicall of repay(market, units, onBehalf) followed by withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, onBehalf, receiver) in the same transaction, where repay increases withdrawable and decreases debt before the collateral withdrawal health check, allowing the borrower to withdraw more collateral than would be safe if the repay had not yet settled, and does the sequencing create any window where debt and collateral are inconsistent?
